Header file for removing previous DUMA and standard C/C++ function definitions and wrappers.

# Purpose
This header file is part of the DUMA (Detect Unintended Memory Access) library, which is a Red-Zone memory allocator. The primary purpose of this file is to prepare the environment for the DUMA library by undefining any previous definitions or wrappers related to memory allocation functions and other DUMA-specific macros. This ensures that the DUMA library can redefine these functions and macros without conflicts, allowing it to monitor and manage memory usage effectively.

The file removes previous definitions for both standard C and C++ memory allocation functions, such as `malloc`, `calloc`, `free`, and their C++ counterparts like `new` and `delete`. It also undefines DUMA-specific macros related to memory alignment, protection, and array handling. This file is intended to be included in application code before other headers to ensure a clean slate for DUMA's memory management functionalities. Some DUMA-specific macros, such as `DUMA_ASSERT` and `DUMA_CHECK`, are retained, indicating their continued use in the application code.
